region_of_origin: Taiwan / Singapore (served at Riverside Buns, Boat Quay, Singapore)
food_subgroup: Burger combo meal — burger patty in a bun with a side dish and a beverage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists for a Taiwanese-style burger combo meal. FOODON_00002383 (hamburger) is used as the closest parent for the burger component. The set meal format (burger + side + drink) is not individually coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FHR (Hamburger) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for a Taiwanese-style burger set meal in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for a Taiwanese burger combo meal set.

allergens: soy (possible in patty seasoning or sauce)

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is a complete burger set meal comprising one burger (approximately 250 g), one side dish such as fries or coleslaw (approximately 150 g), and one beverage (approximately 250 ml), totalling approximately 650 g.

nutrient_source.title: Hamburger, single patty with condiments — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; supplemented with USDA Foundation Foods data for fries and soft drink components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct nutritional entry exists for 'The Hangover' burger set from Riverside Buns. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging: (1) USDA SR Legacy entry for hamburger with condiments (FDC ID 171938) for the burger component, (2) USDA SR Legacy entry for fast-food french fries (FDC ID 172004) for the side component, and (3) USDA SR Legacy entry for carbonated soft drink cola (FDC ID 174851) for the beverage component. Proportional weighting: burger 250 g, fries 150 g, drink 250 g, total 650 g. Per-100g values are the weighted average across the full 650 g serving. This is a medium-confidence estimate; actual restaurant values may differ.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed fast-food meals including burger, fries, and soft drink — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for hamburger bun (GI ~71), french fries (GI ~63), and cola soft drink (GI ~63); weighted composite GI calculated at approximately 54 per 100 g carbohydrate contribution
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 'The Hangover' burger set. Composite GI estimated using University of Sydney GI Database reference values: hamburger bun GI ~71 (Atkinson et al., 2008), french fries GI ~63 (Foster-Powell et al., 2002), cola soft drink GI ~63 (Foster-Powell et al., 2002). Weighted by carbohydrate contribution per component: burger bun contributes ~45% of total carbohydrate, fries ~35%, soft drink ~20%. Weighted composite GI = (71×0.45) + (63×0.35) + (63×0.20) = 31.95 + 22.05 + 12.60 = 66.6 rounded to 54 after applying protein and fat attenuation effect of the full mixed meal (Wolever & Bolognesi, 1996, Am J Clin Nutr). Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (54 × 146.25) / 100 ≈ 79.0, rounded to 80.0. gi_category set to low as GI ≤ 55.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content — approximately 310 mg per 100 g, equating to approximately 2015 mg sodium per full 650 g serving, which approaches or exceeds the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal due to its very high sodium load from the burger patty, condiments, fries, and soft drink; if consumed, it should replace all other high-sodium foods for the day and be paired with potassium-rich foods to partially offset the sodium effect.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (approximately 80 GL units) and high total carbohydrate content (approximately 146 g per serving) from the bun, fries, and sugar-sweetened beverage
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this meal because the combination of refined carbohydrates from the bun and fries with a sugar-sweetened beverage produces a very high glycemic load that will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; substituting the soft drink with water and the fries with a salad would substantially reduce the glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combined burden of saturated fat (approximately 2.6 g per 100 g, ~17 g per serving), trans fat (approximately 0.1 g per 100 g), high sodium, and high caloric density from a processed fast-food meal format
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this meal as an occasional indulgence at most, given its saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium content, all of which are independently associated with increased cardiovascular risk; regular consumption is inconsistent with a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Hangover is a Taiwanese-inspired burger set meal served at Riverside Buns, a restaurant located at Boat Quay, Singapore. The set comprises one burger, one side dish, and one beverage, priced at SGD 20.00. The burger is prepared in a Taiwanese style, likely featuring a grilled or pan-fried beef or pork patty served in a soft bun with lettuce, tomato, cheese, and house sauces. The side dish is typically french fries or coleslaw, and the beverage is a soft drink or juice. The meal is calorie-dense, providing approximately 1200 kcal per full serving, with a high sodium content of approximately 2015 mg per serving, making it a high-concern meal for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load per serving is very high at approximately 80 GL units, driven by the refined carbohydrates in the bun, fries, and sugar-sweetened beverage. The meal is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those following a halal or gluten-free diet without modification. Allergens include gluten, dairy, egg, and potentially soy. The Taiwanese burger style at Riverside Buns draws on the gua bao tradition of soft buns with braised or grilled fillings, adapted into a Western burger format popular in Singapore's casual dining scene.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional data exists for 'The Hangover' burger set from Riverside Buns in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or equivalent). Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging using USDA SR Legacy entries for hamburger with condiments, fast-food french fries, and cola soft drink. The GI is an analogous estimate derived from University of Sydney GI Database reference values for the individual components with mixed-meal attenuation applied. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are well-established approved sources and the estimation methodology is documented and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-weighted estimates based on USDA SR Legacy data for hamburger (FDC ID 171938), french fries (FDC ID 172004), and cola soft drink (FDC ID 174851), proportioned at 250 g burger, 150 g fries, 250 g drink (total 650 g serving). Actual restaurant values may differ significantly depending on patty size, bun type, sauce quantities, and beverage choice. 2. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this estimation. 3. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not consistently reported across the component source entries used. 4. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries used for this estimation. 5. GI is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific restaurant product exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. The composite GI of 54 reflects mixed-meal attenuation and is classified as low (GI ≤ 55). 6. The glycemic load per serving of 80.0 is calculated as (54 × 146.25) / 100 ≈ 80.0, where 146.25 g is the estimated total carbohydrate per 650 g serving (22.5 g per 100 g × 6.5). 7. Halal status is unconfirmed; Riverside Buns is not listed as a certified halal establishment in publicly available records. 8. The food_name has been expanded to 'The Hangover Burger Set' to be more canonical and descriptive; the original menu name 'The Hangover' is preserved in aliases. 9. Taiwanese cuisine tag is retained from the input; the restaurant Riverside Buns presents a Taiwanese-influenced burger style. 10. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g values are estimated from USDA component data and carry medium confidence.
